data.body:
ROUND-3 (post user-correction; corrected block-worthiness = guards a downstream control / high asymmetric cost, NOT code-groundedness). WITHDREW demotion proposal.
TASK2 flip-mechanism: flips are RECALL not FP. 8 flipping same-matfp pairs; decisive would-block findings validity mean 0.992 (min0.86); 14/17 flips = other run MISSED the valid finding, 3 = scored-diff. => valid findings missed, NOT FPs slipping. Lever=finder recall (validity safeguard already working). Cost-justified fix = N-sample FINDER ONLY (union, verify once) => marginal +1x finder not +1x pipeline; can gate to blocking criteria/boundary.
TASK3: 215 findings in 0.60-0.70 band on blocking criteria, all val 0.96-1.0 impact 0.67-0.70 (G6 85,T5e 40,COH 28,E2 28) — high-validity findings just under the bar; adjudicating whether block-worthy (=> lower to 0.60) or fair-advisory.
TASK4 promotion scan (would-block vol / val / impact / repro / revRR): standouts T8(194 wb,85% repro,imp0.80), codebase-grounding G1G2(imp0.80)/E4(imp0.81)/A1, T1(priorart 57pool),T3(feasibility 53pool),E1(coherence). Adjudicating block-worthiness per criterion.
TASK1 deterministic G5: ctx.children (whole child list) ALREADY loaded at orchestrator.py:166-182; pass1.py:427 already counts them. Fix: inject authoritative 'DECOMPOSITION STATE: N children [..]' into G5 finder context + DET guard vetoing a 'flat/undecomposed' G5 finding when children exist. Agent judges CONTENT not COUNT.
CODE-REVIEW evals confirm keep-advisory: perf/db-migrations/security FA=0.0 but findings impact<=0.67 (never block @>=0.70); tests FA0.17, api-compat FA0.33 (would fire on clean diffs if bar lowered). Only DET security blocks.
